activities_projects.title: Theme Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand the deeper theme of universal brotherhood and peace
steps: Read the poem carefully
Identify lines that emphasize equality and shared humanity
Write 3–4 sentences explaining how the poet conveys the theme
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops comprehension and thematic analysis skills

activities_projects.title: Character and Perspective Study
activities_projects.objective: Analyze the poet’s perspective on war and human unity
steps: Identify the poet’s point of view regarding conflict and hate
Write how he wants people to remember the common humanity of all
Explain why this perspective is important
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops understanding of author’s perspective and reasoning

activities_projects.title: Vocabulary Expansion
activities_projects.objective: Learn advanced words and their contextual meanings
steps: Find words like dispossess, defile, outrage, innocence
Write their meanings and synonyms
Use them in sentences reflecting the poem’s theme

activities_projects.title: Cause and Effect Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Understand consequences of war and hatred
steps: Identify lines describing effects of taking arms against each other
Explain what happens to the human earth and humanity
Write 3–4 sentences analyzing the consequences
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops analytical thinking and moral reasoning

activities_projects.title: Figurative Language Identification
activities_projects.objective: Identify literary devices and their effect
steps: Find examples of metaphor, repetition, and imagery
Explain how these devices emphasize unity and shared humanity
Write a short paragraph summarizing your findings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Recognizes poetic techniques and their impact

activities_projects.title: Comparative Reflection
activities_projects.objective: Connect poem’s message to real-world examples
steps: Think of examples from current events or history where unity and understanding were important
Write 3–4 sentences connecting these examples to the poem
Explain the relevance of remembering that no men are foreign
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Relates literature to real-life situations and moral lessons

activities_projects.title: Critical Discussion
steps: Discuss in class why wars and hatred arise despite shared humanity
Write 2–3 key points from the discussion
Explain your own viewpoint with reasons
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops reasoning, discussion, and critical thinking skills
activities_projects.evaluation: Participation and clarity of ideas (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Poetic Tone Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Identify the tone and mood of the poem
steps: Identify words and lines that express caution, reflection, or appeal to conscience
Write 3–4 sentences describing the poem’s tone
Explain how tone supports the poem’s message
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Enhances understanding of tone and mood in poetry

activities_projects.title: Creative Response
steps: Imagine you are addressing people of all nations
Write a short paragraph encouraging peace and understanding
Use examples inspired by the poem
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops creative writing and reflective thinking

activities_projects.title: Illustration Activity
activities_projects.objective: Visualize human unity and shared humanity
steps: Draw people of different nations helping or interacting peacefully
Label your illustration to show the theme of the poem
Share your drawing in class
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Develops creativity and understanding of the poem’s message
